pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 71-73: Bookmark created via Nx Witness REST API POST /rest/v1/devices/{CAMERA_ID}/bookmarks with name='Staff Altercation'

setup_task.sh line 61: BOOKMARK_DURATION_MS=10000 (10 seconds). Confirmed by Nx Witness REST API documentation which uses durationMs parameter in milliseconds. https://support.networkoptix.com/hc/en-us/articles/32894935583895-How-to-Export-Video-Using-the-REST-API

task.json line 5 specifies '10-second buffer before the start and 10-second buffer after the end'. Verified by verifier.py line 67-68 which expects total duration = Bookmark (10s) + Pre (10s) + Post (10s) = 30s

setup_task.sh line 90 and export_result.sh line 7 reference '/home/ga/Documents/evidence_export.mkv'. MKV is a valid Nx Witness export format per https://support.networkoptix.com/hc/en-us/articles/32894935583895-How-to-Export-Video-Using-the-REST-API which states 'webm, mpegts, mpjpeg, mp4, and mkv' are supported archive formats

privileged_info_summary: The task targets the Nx Witness VMS (NetworkOptix video management system) and requires retrieving a bookmark named 'Staff Altercation' with a duration of 10000 milliseconds (10 seconds). The exported video must include a 10-second pre-buffer and 10-second post-buffer, resulting in a total 30-second video file. The file should be exported to /home/ga/Documents/evidence_export.mkv in Matroska format. All metadata values are correctly stated and the data is synthetically generated via test scripts using virtual cameras and FFmpeg-generated video sources.
